title: Tongue-and-Groove Ottoman Tray
summary: A flat serving tray built from ripped tongue-and-groove pine boards edge-joined into a solid panel, framed in mitered 1x2 — a genuinely quick, tool-light small build.
description:
This tray uses tongue-and-groove flooring or paneling stock as its top, which does the edge-joining work for you: the boards interlock and glue up tight with no need for pocket holes or biscuits. One board gets ripped in half lengthwise to fill out the tray's width — a table saw is the clean way to do it, but the plan gives a genuine no-table-saw method: clamp a straight-edge guide offset from your cut line by your circular saw's base-plate offset, then cut with the circular saw riding the guide.

Glue the tongue-and-groove pieces together with the two ripped half-boards on the outside edges, framing the full-width boards in the middle — that arrangement keeps the visible tongue-and-groove detail centered rather than showing a cut edge in the middle of the panel.

Measure the tray's actual assembled width and length for the 1x2 frame pieces rather than trusting a plan number — as with any edge-glued panel, the real dimension after glue-up rarely matches a calculated one exactly. Attach the frame with brad nails and glue, width sides first and then the remaining length sides, for a tray that reads as one clean, framed panel rather than a stack of boards with trim added.

tools.note: Ripping one board in half lengthwise; a circular saw and guide are the stated alternative.

cutList.note: One board ripped in half lengthwise, used on the two outside edges.
Measure your assembled panel and cut to fit.
Measure your assembled panel and cut to fit.

steps.title: Rip the edge board
steps.body: Rip one tongue-and-groove board in half lengthwise on a table saw, or clamp a straight-edge guide (offset from your cut line by your circular saw's base-plate offset) and cut with a circular saw.

steps.title: Glue up the panel
steps.body: Glue the tongue-and-groove pieces together, with the two ripped half-boards on the outside edges.

steps.title: Attach the width sides
steps.body: Measure the width of the assembled tray panel and cut two 1x2s to match. Attach with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Attach the length sides and finish
steps.body: Measure the remaining edges of the tray and cut two 1x2s to match. Attach with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue. Sand, finish, and add handles if desired.

unresolvedImages.alt: Flat serving tray built from tongue-and-groove boards
